summary: Matched the common fighter Furafura action by moving five functions out of asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s into new C source/header files. The PR added ftCo_Furafura.c with ftCo_80099010, ftCo_Furafura_Anim, IASA, Phys, and Coll, wired the object into obj_files.mk, moved the ftCo_80099010 declaration out of the broad ft_08A4.h header, and exposed the Furafura header to ShieldBreakStand. It also refined ftCommonData fields at offsets 0x2F8-0x304 and corrected ft_800885A8's prototype/call sites to accept three integer arguments. The match report states the new file and all listed functions reached 100.00%.
what_changed: Removed the assembly implementations of ftCo_80099010, ftCo_Furafura_Anim, ftCo_Furafura_IASA, ftCo_Furafura_Phys, and ftCo_Furafura_Coll from asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Furafura.c implementing the Furafura action transition, animation, IASA, physics, and collision callbacks.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Furafura.h with address-commented declarations and include guard GALE01_099010.
Added ftCo_Furafura.c.o to TEXT_O_FILES in obj_files.mk before the remaining ft_08A4.s object, so the newly matched C code is built.
Updated ftCommonData in src/melee/ft/types.h to split the old x2F4 byte blob into x2F4 plus floats x2F8, x2FC, x300, and x304, which are used by the Furafura logic.
Changed ft_800885A8's declaration from a single Fighter* argument to Fighter*, int, int, int, and propagated the extra arguments through two ftaction.c call sites.
Moved the ftCo_80099010 declaration out of src/melee/ft/ft_08A4.h and made ShieldBreakStand include ftCo_Furafura.h directly.
Deleted local sdata2 constants .L_804D8670 and .L_804D8674 from the asm file because the matched C emitted/used the needed constants.
smart_moves: Kept the matched action in a dedicated ftCommon action file rather than leaving the declaration in the generic ft_08A4.h bucket, improving symbol locality.
Used the action-specific header ftCo_Furafura.h from ftCo_ShieldBreakStand.c, which makes dependencies clearer than relying on the broad ft_08A4.h declaration.
Recovered concrete ftCommonData float fields at offsets 0x2F8, 0x2FC, 0x300, and 0x304 based on direct uses in the decompiled Furafura logic, reducing anonymous padding.
Corrected ft_800885A8's prototype by following observed call arguments, then updated existing ftaction dispatch call sites so the declaration and uses stayed consistent.
For matching ftCo_Furafura_Anim, explicitly documented and gated an unused stack workaround with MUST_MATCH and a todo, preserving match behavior without pretending the artifact has semantic value.
decomp_lessons: When lifting a small action group from a larger asm file, add the new C object immediately before the remaining asm object in obj_files.mk so the link order continues to match.
Use action-specific headers for ftCommon action callbacks as symbols are split out; avoid keeping newly matched symbols only in monolithic unknown headers.
Do not leave struct ranges as anonymous byte arrays when a matched function gives clear typed offset evidence; split out named or placeholder fields at exact offsets.

Prototype recovery can require updating unrelated existing call sites. If a callee is discovered to take more parameters, fix both the header and all dispatch paths that were previously compiling through an incomplete prototype.
Empty callbacks can be represented as empty C functions when the original assembly is just blr, as shown by ftCo_Furafura_IASA.
Thin wrapper callbacks such as Phys and Coll can match cleanly by directly forwarding to existing fighter helpers when the original assembly only sets up a stack frame and branches to the helper.
naming_conventions: Common fighter action files use the ftCo_ prefix and action name in CamelCase after the prefix, e.g. ftCo_Furafura.c and ftCo_Furafura.h.
Action callback names follow the pattern ftCo_<Action>_Anim, ftCo_<Action>_IASA, ftCo_<Action>_Phys, and ftCo_<Action>_Coll.
Unknown entry functions may remain address-based, e.g. ftCo_80099010, until their role is confidently named.
Header include guards in this slice use the address-oriented GALE01_099010 style.
ftCommonData placeholder fields retain offset-derived names such as x2F8, x2FC, x300, and x304 when semantic names are not yet known.
Fighter fields shown in the matched logic include existing semantic and offset-style names together, such as shield_health, dmg.x1830_percent, and x1A4C.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The transition function matched a Fighter_ChangeMotionState call using ftCo_MS_Furafura and flags Ft_MF_SkipModel | Ft_MF_SkipMatAnim with start frame NULL/0 and rate 1.
The Furafura setup logic clamps p_ftCommonData->x2F8 - fp->dmg.x1830_percent to zero before passing percent + p_ftCommonData->x2FC into ftCommon_8007DBCC.

The animation callback refreshes shield_health from p_ftCommonData->x280_unkShieldHealth, decrements fp->x1A4C by p_ftCommonData->x300, calls ftCommon_8007DC08 with x304, and exits to ft_8008A2BC when x1A4C <= 0.
The match used an #ifdef MUST_MATCH local u8 _[8] = { 0 } stack artifact in ftCo_Furafura_Anim to account for unused stack behavior.
ftCo_Furafura_Phys matched as a direct wrapper around ft_80084F3C(gobj).
ftCo_Furafura_Coll matched as a direct wrapper around ft_80083F88(gobj).
ftCo_Furafura_IASA matched as an empty function corresponding to a bare blr.
The 100.00% report covered the file plus ftCo_80099010, ftCo_Furafura_Anim, ftCo_Furafura_Coll, ftCo_Furafura_Phys, and ftCo_Furafura_IASA.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename ftCo_80099010 to a semantic Furafura entry function name.
Search for later struct cleanup around ftCommonData fields x2F4, x2F8, x2FC, x300, and x304.
Search for later prototype or implementation work on ft_800885A8 now that its three integer parameters are exposed.
Check whether the MUST_MATCH unused stack workaround in ftCo_Furafura_Anim was later removed or replaced with a cleaner matching idiom.
